reasoning: Given the end of Russian pipeline deliveries through Ukraine in January 2025, European natural gas imports from Russia lack the physical capacity to reach 15 billion cubic meters (bcm) in a single quarter. In 2024, when the Ukraine route was still active, total imports summed to ~52 bcm (or ~13 bcm per quarter). Recent daily import rates in February 2025 equate to roughly 11.7 bcm per quarter, primarily from TurkStream and LNG. The maximum capacity of the TurkStream pipeline directed to Europe is approximately 15.75 bcm per year, and peak LNG imports are structurally constrained, making 15 bcm in one quarter virtually impossible. The extremely low market estimate of 1% (0.01) heavily corroborates this, indicating that Q1-Q3 2025 data fell well short of the threshold and there is no expectation of Q4 doing so. I am forecasting the minimum allowable probability of 0.02.
batch_queries: Will the EU import at least 15 billion cubic meters of natural gas from Russia in any quarter of 2025?
